description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a circular stone base constructed from irregular, dark gray stones, giving it a rustic and sturdy appearance. The well is topped with a pitched wooden roof made of brown planks, supported by two vertical wooden posts. A horizontal wooden beam spans the posts, holding a hand crank mechanism with a metal handle for drawing water. A wooden bucket is placed nearby, suggesting its use for retrieving water from the well. The interior of the well is open, revealing a dark, hollow space where water would be accessed. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements, and the structure appears robust and weather-resistant.
description: A rustic stone water well with a pitched wooden roof, hand crank, and a wooden bucket nearby. The structure is sturdy and functional, with a dark stone base and wooden elements.
description_view_0: Front view showing the stone well, wooden roof, hand crank, and a bucket to the right.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the triangular profile of the wooden roof and the bucket positioned in front.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, but the bucket is now on the left and the crank is visible on the right.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the back of the roof and the well, with no crank or bucket visible.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: A hand crank mechanism with a metal handle and a wooden bucket is present, attached to a horizontal wooden beam.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: Circular stone wall approximately 1 meter high surrounds the well, with a wooden roof supported by posts.
